metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1700
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors use light microscopy to localize syndapin/PACSIN in mouse photoreceptor cells. The protein is found in the synaptic layers of the retina in close proximity to the synaptic ribbon, the release site in these neurons. Figure 5 uses standard confocal microscopy and figure 6 uses super resolution SIM microscopy.

15/11/2017 Pim
- For PMID:23785143 I allow annotation to term 'presynaptic endocytic zone' because the authors claim this as an experimental finding. The title states "A local, periactive zone endocytic machinery at photoreceptor synapses in close vicinity to synaptic ribbons". Experimental description: From legend to figure 5, "Distribution of amphiphysin and syndapin in photoreceptor ribbon synapses at high resolution (conventional imaging). A, B, The 0.5 μm thin sections of the mouse retina..."
